%% Helper function to check if a single number X is "good"
is_good(X, Directions) ->
    % 1. Convert X to 16-digit string, padded with leading zeros
    % We convert X to binary string and pad it.
    StringX = to_binary_string(X),
    PaddedString = pad_left(StringX, 16, "0"),

    % 2. Extract digits and fill 4x4 grid (list of 4 lists)
    Digits = get_digits(PaddedString),
    Grid = create_grid(Digits),

    % 3. Simulate path traversal
    % Start at (0, 0). Sequence starts with Grid[0][0].
    StartDigit = Grid !! 0 !! 0,
    InitialSequence = [StartDigit],
    
    % Path traversal using fold/recursion
    Sequence = traverse_path(Grid, Directions, [0, 0], InitialSequence),

    % 4. Check monotonicity
    is_non_decreasing(Sequence).

% Converts an integer X (which can be large) to a string representation.
to_binary_string(X) ->
    case integer_to_list(X) of
        [] -> "0"; % Handle case X=0 if it were allowed, though L>=1
        List -> lists:reverse(lists:map(fun(Digit) -> integer_to_list(Digit) end, List))
    end.

% Pads a string on the left with a specified character up to a target length.
pad_left(Str, Length, PadChar) ->
    Len = length(Str),
    Missing = Length - Len,
    case Missing of
        N when N > 0 -> list_to_binary(list_to_string(N, PadChar), Str);
        _ -> Str
    end.

% Gets the individual digits (as integers 0-9) from a string.
get_digits(Str) ->
    lists:map(fun(Char) -> char_to_integer(Char) end, Str).

% Creates a 4x4 grid (list of 4 lists of 4 integers)
create_grid(Digits) ->
    [
        lists:split_at(4, Digits) !! 0,
        lists:split_at(4, Digits) !! 1,
        lists:split_at(4, Digits) !! 2,
        lists:split_at(4, Digits) !! 3
    ].

% Recursively traverses the path and builds the sequence.
% Grid: 4x4 array of digits.
% Directions: list of direction characters.
% CurrentPos: [Row, Col].
% CurrentSequence: list of digits collected so far.
traverse_path(Grid, [], _Pos, Sequence) ->
    Sequence;
traverse_path(Grid, [Dir | Rest], [R, C], Sequence) ->
    NewPos = case Dir of
        <<"D">> -> [R + 1, C];
        <<"R">> -> [R, C + 1];
        _ -> [R, C] % Should not happen based on problem constraints
    end,
    
    % Assuming movement is always within bounds 0-3 due to 3D/3R movement from (0,0)
    NewRow = NewPos !! 0,
    NewCol = NewPos !! 1,
    
    NextDigit = Grid !! NewRow !! NewCol,
    NewSequence = Sequence ++ [NextDigit],
    
    traverse_path(Grid, Rest, NewPos, NewSequence).

% Checks if a list of integers is non-decreasing.
is_non_decreasing([]) -> true;
is_non_decreasing([_]) -> true;
is_non_decreasing([Head | Tail]) ->
    check_recursive(Head, Tail).

check_recursive(_Prev, []).
check_recursive(Prev, [Current | Rest]) when Prev <= Current ->
    check_recursive(Current, Rest);
check_recursive(_, _) ->
    false.

%% Main solution function
solve(L, R, Directions) ->
    % Initialize count
    Count = 0,
    
    % Iterate from L to R using recursion
    Count = iterate_range(L, R, Directions, Count).

% Recursive function to iterate and count good numbers
iterate_range(Current, R, Directions, Count) when Current > R ->
    Count;
iterate_range(Current, R, Directions, Count) ->
    case is_good(Current, Directions) of
        true ->
            NewCount = Count + 1;
        false ->
            NewCount = Count
    end,
    
    % Next number (Handle large number increment)
    NextCurrent = Current + 1,
    iterate_range(NextCurrent, R, Directions, NewCount).
```

    const H = 4;
    const GRID_SIZE = W * H; // 16

    // --- Step 1: Calculate Path Indices ---

    let r_curr = 0;
    let c_curr = 0;
    // Path starts at (0, 0)

    for (const dir of directions) {
        let r_next = r_curr;
        let c_next = c_curr;
        
        if (dir === 'D') {
            r_next += 1;
        } else if (dir === 'R') {
            c_next += 1;
        }

        // Clamp coordinates to stay within the 4x4 grid boundaries [0, 3]
        r_next = Math.min(H - 1, Math.max(0, r_next));
        c_next = Math.min(W - 1, Math.max(0, c_next));

        r_curr = r_next;
        c_curr = c_next;
        positions.push([r_curr, c_curr]);

    // J_k: the 0-indexed position (0..15) in the 16-digit string corresponding to S_k.

     * @param {bigint} N_bigint - The upper bound (inclusive).
     * @returns {bigint} Count of good integers <= N.

        if (N_bigint < 0n) return 0n;
        
        // Convert N to 16-digit string representation (MSD first)
        // Since N <= 9*10^15, it might have fewer than 16 digits. Pad to 16.
        let N_str = N_bigint.toString();
        while (N_str.length < 16) {
            N_str = '0' + N_str;
        }

        // Memoization table: memo[j][tight][last_val]
        // j: 0..15 (16)
        // tight: 0..1 (2)
        // last_val: 0..9 (10)

        const memo = new Array(16).fill(0).map(() =>

         * @param {number} last_val - The value of the last constrained digit S_k encountered (0-9).
         * @returns {bigint} Count of ways to complete the suffix.
         */
        const dp = (j, tight, last_val) => {
            if (j === 16) {
                return 1n; // Found one valid number
            }

            if (memo[j][tight][last_val] !== -1n) {
                return memo[j][tight][last_val];
            }

            let count = 0n;

            for (let d = 0; d <= upper_bound; d++) {
                // 1. Determine the next state
                const new_tight = tight && (d === upper_bound) ? 1 : 0;
                let new_last_val = last_val;
                let is_valid = true;

                // 2. Check constraints if this digit d is part of the path sequence S
                const k_new = J_map[j];

                        // S_{k_new - 1} was represented by last_val

                        }

                    }

        };

        return dp(0, 1, 0);
    };

    // Result = Count(R) - Count(L-1)
    const countR = count_le(r);

%% Helper function to check if a sequence of numbers is non-decreasing
is_non_decreasing(List) ->
    case List of
        [_] -> true;
        [] -> true;
        [H1, H2 | Rest] ->
            if H1 <= H2 -> is_non_decreasing([H2 | Rest]);
            true -> false
            end
    end.

%% Helper function to simulate the path and get the sequence for a given number X
get_path_sequence(X, Directions) ->
    % 1. Convert X to 16-digit padded string representation
    DigitsString = integer_to_list(X, 16),
    % Pad with leading zeros if necessary (though integer_to_list handles padding if length is specified correctly,
    % it's safer to explicitly ensure 16 digits for large numbers if X was passed as a string/large number type)
    % Since X is an Erlang integer, we use string conversion and padding.
    XString = io_lib:format("~w", [X]),
    PaddedString = case string:length(XString) of
        L when L < 16 -> string:pad_left(XString, 16, "0");
        _ -> XString
    end,

    % 2. Fill the 4x4 grid (16 digits)
    Grid = [
        [string:at(PaddedString, 1), string:at(PaddedString, 2), string:at(PaddedString, 3), string:at(PaddedString, 4)],
        [string:at(PaddedString, 5), string:at(PaddedString, 6), string:at(PaddedString, 7), string:at(PaddedString, 8)],
        [string:at(PaddedString, 9), string:at(PaddedString, 10), string:at(PaddedString, 11), string:at(PaddedString, 12)],
        [string:at(PaddedString, 13), string:at(PaddedString, 14), string:at(PaddedString, 15), string:at(PaddedString, 16)]
    ],
    
    % Convert characters (digits) to integers
    GridInt = lists:map(fun(Row) -> lists:map(fun(Char) -> string:to_integer(Char) end, Row) end, Grid),

    % 3. Path Tracing
    
    % Start at (0, 0). Initial value.
    StartDigit = GridInt !! 0 !! 0,
    
    % Initialize state: {Row, Column, Accumulated_Path}
    InitialState = {0, 0, [StartDigit]},
    
    % Fold over directions to simulate 6 moves
    FinalState = lists:foldl(fun({R, C, Path}, Direction) ->
        NewR = R,
        NewC = C,
        
        case Direction of
            <<"D">> -> NewR = R + 1;
            <<"R">> -> NewC = C + 1;
            _ -> {R, C, Path} % Should not happen
        end,
        
        % Boundary check is implicitly handled by grid dimensions 0..3
        if NewR >= 0 and NewR < 4 and NewC >= 0 and NewC < 4 ->
            NewDigit = GridInt !! NewR !! NewC,
            {NewR, NewC, [NewDigit | Path]}
        else
            % If movement goes out of bounds, we stop recording digits, but for safety,
            % we should stick to the simulation path defined by the problem.
            % Since the problem doesn't specify behavior outside 4x4, we assume valid moves keep us inside.
            {NewR, NewC, Path} 
        end
    end, InitialState, Directions),

    % The path was accumulated in reverse order in the foldl
    {_, _, ReversedPath} = FinalState,
    lists:reverse(ReversedPath).

%% Main solver function
solve(L, R, Directions) ->
    Count = 0,
    
    % Iterate from L to R
    Count = lists:foldl(fun(Acc, X) ->
        Sequence = get_path_sequence(X, Directions),
        if is_non_decreasing(Sequence) -> Acc + 1;
        true -> Acc
        end
    end, 0, lists:seq(L, R)),
    
    Count.

/**
 * @param {bigint} l
 * @param {bigint} r
 * @param {string} directions
 * @returns {bigint}
 */
function solve(l, r, directions) {

    const W = 4;
    const H = 4;
    const GRID_SIZE = W * H; // 16

    // --- Step 1: Calculate Path Indices ---

    let r_curr = 0;
    let c_curr = 0;
    // Path starts at (0, 0)
    const positions = [[0, 0]]; 

    for (const dir of directions) {
        let r_next = r_curr;
        let c_next = c_curr;
        
        if (dir === 'D') {
            r_next += 1;
        } else if (dir === 'R') {
            c_next += 1;
        }

        // Clamp coordinates to stay within the 4x4 grid boundaries [0, 3]
        r_next = Math.min(H - 1, Math.max(0, r_next));
        c_next = Math.min(W - 1, Math.max(0, c_next));

        r_curr = r_next;
        c_curr = c_next;
        positions.push([r_curr, c_curr]);
    }
    
    // positions now contains 7 pairs: P_0, P_1, ..., P_6

    // J_k: the 0-indexed position (0..15) in the 16-digit string corresponding to S_k.
    const J_k = new Array(7); 
    for (let k = 0; k < 7; k++) {
        const [r, c] = positions[k];
        // Row-major indexing: index = r * W + c
        J_k[k] = r * W + c;
    }

    // J_map[j]: stores k if index j (0..15) corresponds to S_k, otherwise -1.
    const J_map = new Array(GRID_SIZE).fill(-1);
    for (let k = 0; k < 7; k++) {
        const j = J_k[k];
        J_map[j] = k;
    }

    // --- Step 2: Digit DP Setup ---
    
    /**
     * Counts numbers x <= N_bigint such that x is a "good" integer.
     * @param {bigint} N_bigint - The upper bound (inclusive).
     * @returns {bigint} Count of good integers <= N.
     */
    const count_le = (N_bigint) => {
        if (N_bigint < 0n) return 0n;
        
        // Convert N to 16-digit string representation (MSD first)
        // Since N <= 9*10^15, it might have fewer than 16 digits. Pad to 16.
        let N_str = N_bigint.toString();
        while (N_str.length < 16) {
            N_str = '0' + N_str;
        }

        // Memoization table: memo[j][tight][last_val]
        // j: 0..15 (16)
        // tight: 0..1 (2)
        // last_val: 0..9 (10)
        // We use 10 states for last_val (0-9), handling the initial state implicitly/by setting a default minimum.
        const memo = new Array(16).fill(0).map(() => 
            new Array(2).fill(0).map(() => 
                new Array(10).fill(-1n)
            )
        );

        /**
         * DP recursive function.
         * @param {number} j - Current digit index (0 to 15).
         * @param {number} tight - Tight constraint flag (1 if restricted by N_str).
         * @param {number} last_val - The value of the last constrained digit S_k encountered (0-9).
         * @returns {bigint} Count of ways to complete the suffix.
         */
        const dp = (j, tight, last_val) => {
            if (j === 16) {
                return 1n; // Found one valid number
            }

            if (memo[j][tight][last_val] !== -1n) {
                return memo[j][tight][last_val];
            }

            const upper_bound = tight ? parseInt(N_str[j]) : 9;
            let count = 0n;

            for (let d = 0; d <= upper_bound; d++) {
                // 1. Determine the next state
                const new_tight = tight && (d === upper_bound) ? 1 : 0;
                let new_last_val = last_val;
                let is_valid = true;

                // 2. Check constraints if this digit d is part of the path sequence S
                const k_new = J_map[j]; 

                if (k_new !== -1) {
                    // This digit d is S_{k_new}
                    
                    if (k_new > 0) {
                        // Constraint: S_{k_new} >= S_{k_new - 1}
                        // S_{k_new - 1} was represented by last_val
                        if (d < last_val) {
                            is_valid = false;
                        }
                    } 
                    
                    // Update last_val if this digit is part of the sequence
                    if (is_valid) {
                        new_last_val = d;
                    }
                }

                if (is_valid) {
                    count += dp(j + 1, new_tight ? 1 : 0, new_last_val);
                }
            }

            memo[j][tight][last_val] = count;
            return count;
        };

        // Initial call: j=0, tight=1 (restricted by N), last_val=0 (since digits are non-negative, 0 acts as the minimum constraint for S0).
        return dp(0, 1, 0);
    };

    // --- Step 3: Final Calculation ---

    // Result = Count(R) - Count(L-1)
    const countR = count_le(r);
    const countL_minus_1 = count_le(l - 1n);

    return countR - countL_minus_1;
}

function solve(l, r, directions) {

    const H = 4;
    const GRID_SIZE = W * H; // 16

    // --- Step 1: Calculate Path Indices ---

    let r_curr = 0;
    let c_curr = 0;
    // Path starts at (0, 0)
    const positions = [[0, 0]]; 

    for (const dir of directions) {
        let r_next = r_curr;
        let c_next = c_curr;
        
        if (dir === 'D') {
            r_next += 1;
        } else if (dir === 'R') {
            c_next += 1;
        }

        // Clamp coordinates to stay within the 4x4 grid boundaries [0, 3]
        r_next = Math.min(H - 1, Math.max(0, r_next));
        c_next = Math.min(W - 1, Math.max(0, c_next));

        r_curr = r_next;
        c_curr = c_next;
        positions.push([r_curr, c_curr]);
    }

    // J_k: the 0-indexed position (0..15) in the 16-digit string corresponding to S_k.

    for (let k = 0; k < 7; k++) {

        J_k[k] = r * W + c;

        J_map[j] = k;

     * @param {bigint} N_bigint - The upper bound (inclusive).
     * @returns {bigint} Count of good integers <= N.
     */
    const count_le = (N_bigint) => {
        if (N_bigint < 0n) return 0n;
        
        // Convert N to 16-digit string representation (MSD first)
        // Since N <= 9*10^15, it might have fewer than 16 digits. Pad to 16.
        let N_str = N_bigint.toString();
        while (N_str.length < 16) {
            N_str = '0' + N_str;
        }

        // Memoization table: memo[j][tight][last_val]
        // j: 0..15 (16)
        // tight: 0..1 (2)
        // last_val: 0..9 (10)
        // We use 10 states for last_val (0-9), handling the initial state implicitly/by setting a default minimum.
        const memo = new Array(16).fill(0).map(() => 
            new Array(2).fill(0).map(() => 
                new Array(10).fill(-1n)
            )
        );

         * @param {number} j - Current digit index (0 to 15).
         * @param {number} tight - Tight constraint flag (1 if restricted by N_str).
         * @param {number} last_val - The value of the last constrained digit S_k encountered (0-9).
         * @returns {bigint} Count of ways to complete the suffix.
         */
        const dp = (j, tight, last_val) => {
            if (j === 16) {
                return 1n; // Found one valid number
            }

            if (memo[j][tight][last_val] !== -1n) {
                return memo[j][tight][last_val];
            }

            const upper_bound = tight ? parseInt(N_str[j]) : 9;
            let count = 0n;

            for (let d = 0; d <= upper_bound; d++) {
                // 1. Determine the next state
                const new_tight = tight && (d === upper_bound) ? 1 : 0;
                let new_last_val = last_val;
                let is_valid = true;

                // 2. Check constraints if this digit d is part of the path sequence S
                const k_new = J_map[j]; 

                if (k_new !== -1) {
                    // This digit d is S_{k_new}
                    
                    if (k_new > 0) {
                        // Constraint: S_{k_new} >= S_{k_new - 1}
                        // S_{k_new - 1} was represented by last_val
                        if (d < last_val) {
                            is_valid = false;
                        }
                    } 
                    
                    // Update last_val if this digit is part of the sequence
                    if (is_valid) {
                        new_last_val = d;
                    }
                }

                if (is_valid) {
                    count += dp(j + 1, new_tight ? 1 : 0, new_last_val);
                }
            }

            return count;
        };

        // Initial call: j=0, tight=1 (restricted by N), last_val=0 (since digits are non-negative, 0 acts as the minimum constraint for S0).
        return dp(0, 1, 0);
    };

    const countR = count_le(r);

}

 * @param {bigint} r
 * @param {string} directions
 * @returns {bigint}
 */
function solve(l, r, directions) {

    const W = 4;
    const H = 4;
    const GRID_SIZE = W * H; // 16

    // --- Step 1: Calculate Path Indices ---

    let r_curr = 0;
    let c_curr = 0;
    // Path starts at (0, 0)
    const positions = [[0, 0]]; 

    for (const dir of directions) {
        let r_next = r_curr;
        let c_next = c_curr;
        
        if (dir === 'D') {
            r_next += 1;
        } else if (dir === 'R') {
            c_next += 1;
        }

        // Clamp coordinates to stay within the 4x4 grid boundaries [0, 3]
        r_next = Math.min(H - 1, Math.max(0, r_next));
        c_next = Math.min(W - 1, Math.max(0, c_next));

        r_curr = r_next;
        c_curr = c_next;
        positions.push([r_curr, c_curr]);
    }
    
    // positions now contains 7 pairs: P_0, P_1, ..., P_6

    // J_k: the 0-indexed position (0..15) in the 16-digit string corresponding to S_k.
    const J_k = new Array(7); 
    for (let k = 0; k < 7; k++) {
        const [r, c] = positions[k];
        // Row-major indexing: index = r * W + c
        J_k[k] = r * W + c;
    }

    // J_map[j]: stores k if index j (0..15) corresponds to S_k, otherwise -1.
    const J_map = new Array(GRID_SIZE).fill(-1);
    for (let k = 0; k < 7; k++) {
        const j = J_k[k];
        J_map[j] = k;
    }

    // --- Step 2: Digit DP Setup ---
    
    /**
     * Counts numbers x <= N_bigint such that x is a "good" integer.
     * @param {bigint} N_bigint - The upper bound (inclusive).
     * @returns {bigint} Count of good integers <= N.
     */
    const count_le = (N_bigint) => {
        if (N_bigint < 0n) return 0n;
        
        // Convert N to 16-digit string representation (MSD first)
        // Since N <= 9*10^15, it might have fewer than 16 digits. Pad to 16.
        let N_str = N_bigint.toString();
        while (N_str.length < 16) {
            N_str = '0' + N_str;
        }

        // Memoization table: memo[j][tight][last_val]
        // j: 0..15 (16)
        // tight: 0..1 (2)
        // last_val: 0..9 (10)
        // We use 10 states for last_val (0-9), handling the initial state implicitly/by setting a default minimum.
        const memo = new Array(16).fill(0).map(() => 
            new Array(2).fill(0).map(() => 
                new Array(10).fill(-1n)
            )
        );

        /**
         * DP recursive function.
         * @param {number} j - Current digit index (0 to 15).
         * @param {number} tight - Tight constraint flag (1 if restricted by N_str).
         * @param {number} last_val - The value of the last constrained digit S_k encountered (0-9).
         * @returns {bigint} Count of ways to complete the suffix.
         */
        const dp = (j, tight, last_val) => {
            if (j === 16) {
                return 1n; // Found one valid number
            }

            if (memo[j][tight][last_val] !== -1n) {
                return memo[j][tight][last_val];
            }

            const upper_bound = tight ? parseInt(N_str[j]) : 9;
            let count = 0n;

            for (let d = 0; d <= upper_bound; d++) {
                // 1. Determine the next state
                const new_tight = tight && (d === upper_bound) ? 1 : 0;
                let new_last_val = last_val;
                let is_valid = true;

                // 2. Check constraints if this digit d is part of the path sequence S
                const k_new = J_map[j]; 

                if (k_new !== -1) {
                    // This digit d is S_{k_new}
                    
                    if (k_new > 0) {
                        // Constraint: S_{k_new} >= S_{k_new - 1}
                        // S_{k_new - 1} was represented by last_val
                        if (d < last_val) {
                            is_valid = false;
                        }
                    } 
                    
                    // Update last_val if this digit is part of the sequence
                    if (is_valid) {
                        new_last_val = d;
                    }
                }

                if (is_valid) {
                    count += dp(j + 1, new_tight ? 1 : 0, new_last_val);
                }
            }

            memo[j][tight][last_val] = count;
            return count;
        };

        // Initial call: j=0, tight=1 (restricted by N), last_val=0 (since digits are non-negative, 0 acts as the minimum constraint for S0).
        return dp(0, 1, 0);
    };

    // --- Step 3: Final Calculation ---

    // Result = Count(R) - Count(L-1)
    const countR = count_le(r);
    const countL_minus_1 = count_le(l - 1n);

    return countR - countL_minus_1;
}

    const H = 4;
    const GRID_SIZE = W * H; // 16

    // --- Step 1: Calculate Path Indices ---

    let r_curr = 0;
    let c_curr = 0;
    // Path starts at (0, 0)

    for (const dir of directions) {
        let r_next = r_curr;
        let c_next = c_curr;
        
        if (dir === 'D') {
            r_next += 1;
        } else if (dir === 'R') {
            c_next += 1;
        }

        c_next = Math.min(W - 1, Math.max(0, c_next));

        r_curr = r_next;
        c_curr = c_next;
        positions.push([r_curr, c_curr]);

    // J_k: the 0-indexed position (0..15) in the 16-digit string corresponding to S_k.

     * @param {bigint} N_bigint - The upper bound (inclusive).
     * @returns {bigint} Count of good integers <= N.

        if (N_bigint < 0n) return 0n;
        
        // Convert N to 16-digit string representation (MSD first)
        // Since N <= 9*10^15, it might have fewer than 16 digits. Pad to 16.
        let N_str = N_bigint.toString();
        while (N_str.length < 16) {
            N_str = '0' + N_str;
        }

        // Memoization table: memo[j][tight][last_val]
        // j: 0..15 (16)

        // last_val: 0..9 (10)

        const memo = new Array(16).fill(0).map(() =>

         * @returns {bigint} Count of ways to complete the suffix.
         */
        const dp = (j, tight, last_val) => {
            if (j === 16) {
                return 1n; // Found one valid number
            }

            if (memo[j][tight][last_val] !== -1n) {
                return memo[j][tight][last_val];
            }

            const upper_bound = tight ? parseInt(N_str[j]) : 9;
            let count = 0n;

            for (let d = 0; d <= upper_bound; d++) {
                // 1. Determine the next state
                const new_tight = tight && (d === upper_bound) ? 1 : 0;
                let new_last_val = last_val;
                let is_valid = true;

                // 2. Check constraints if this digit d is part of the path sequence S
                const k_new = J_map[j]; 

                if (k_new !== -1) {
                    // This digit d is S_{k_new}
                    
                    if (k_new > 0) {
                        // Constraint: S_{k_new} >= S_{k_new - 1}
                        // S_{k_new - 1} was represented by last_val
                        if (d < last_val) {
                            is_valid = false;
                        }
                    }

                        new_last_val = d;
                    }
                }

                }

        };

        return dp(0, 1, 0);
